title: verbascum × humnickii
intro: Verbascum × humnickii, also known as Hummell's mullein, is a hybrid plant species that is a cross between Verbascum thapsus and Verbascum nigrum, and is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, with its leaves, flowers, and roots containing medicinal compounds such as mucilage, saponins, and flavonoids. The health benefits of Verbascum × humnickii include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant can be dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be taken internally to support respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
